meta.title: HELIX plan: authority-ordered gaps, ready for the next mode

narration: Brief and PRD are aligned. Run HELIX align in plan mode for an authority-ordered gap list — each one handing off cleanly to the next workflow.

prompt: Use HELIX align on this project and produce an authority-ordered plan. For each non-aligned gap, name the destination artifact type, the deliverable shape, the suggested next workflow mode, and evidence references.

narration: Every finding carries four fields — destination, deliverable, next mode, evidence — so the next agent can pick it up without re-reading the whole tree.

assistant:
# HELIX Alignment Report — claims-ingest

Scope: Discover + Frame artifacts present; Architecture, Design, Test Plan, Implementation, and Code are absent.

## Authority Map (what exists)

| Layer              | Artifact                                | Status                       |
|--------------------|-----------------------------------------|------------------------------|
| Discover / Vision  | 00-discover/product-vision.md           | Present                      |
| PRD                | 01-frame/prd.md (ddx:claims-ingest.prd) | Present                      |
| Feature spec       | 01-frame/features/FEAT-001-csv-ingest   | Present (stories embedded)   |
| Project concerns   | 01-frame/concerns.md                    | Present                      |
| Architecture/ADRs  | —                                       | Missing                      |
| Technical Design   | —                                       | Missing                      |
| Test Plan          | —                                       | Missing                      |
| Implementation     | —                                       | Missing                      |

## ALIGNED findings

- Vision "CSV in, Parquet out" ↔ PRD R-1..R-4 (vision:5-7 ↔ prd.md:15-32).
- PRD R-1 header sniffing ↔ FEAT-001 US-INGEST-3 (prd.md:17-20 ↔ FEAT-001:17-18).
- PRD R-2 validation/quarantine ↔ FEAT-001 US-INGEST-2 (prd.md:22-25 ↔ FEAT-001:14-16).
- PRD R-4 operator CLI ↔ FEAT-001 US-INGEST-1 (prd.md:29-32 ↔ FEAT-001:11-13).

## Non-aligned gaps (authority-ordered)

### G1 — Vision audit-trail commitment is unrequired downstream
- Classification:    INCOMPLETE
- Evidence:          product-vision.md:5-7, :17-19; no matching requirement in prd.md.
- Destination:       PRD
- Deliverable:       New requirement (R-6 "Lineage/audit fields") — per-row provenance columns + queryability promise.
- Next workflow mode: frame

### G2 — Vision latency SLA is unrequired downstream
- Classification:    UNDERSPECIFIED
- Evidence:          product-vision.md:17-19 ("under fifteen minutes"); prd.md R-5 specifies throughput only.
- Destination:       PRD
- Deliverable:       New requirement (arrival → row visibility ≤15 min, p95), distinct from R-5 throughput.
- Next workflow mode: frame

### G3 — PRD names "deduplicates" but no requirement defines the dedup contract
- Classification:    UNDERSPECIFIED
- Evidence:          prd.md:10-12 (summary names dedup); :41-42 (single-file dedup in scope) — no R-* defines key/ordering.
- Destination:       PRD
- Deliverable:       New requirement: in-file dedup key (e.g. claim_id), tie-break rule, quarantine-vs-collapse policy.
- Next workflow mode: frame

### G4 — Observability KPIs surface in FEAT but not PRD
- Classification:    INCOMPLETE
- Evidence:          FEAT-001:19-20 (US-INGEST-4 counters); no observability requirement in prd.md.
- Destination:       PRD
- Deliverable:       New requirement covering operator metrics (throughput, rejection rate, latency).
- Next workflow mode: frame

### G5 — FEAT-001 "Out of scope" cites PRD non-goals that do not exist
- Classification:    DIVERGENT
- Evidence:          FEAT-001:30-32 ("OAuth, sessions, JWTs — see PRD non-goals") vs prd.md:38-42 (non-goals are streaming + cross-payer dedup).
- Destination:       FEAT-001
- Deliverable:       Rewrite the "Out of scope" block — drop the OAuth/JWT line or echo real PRD non-goals.
- Next workflow mode: validate

### G7 — No Architecture / ADRs for stack choices
- Classification:    INCOMPLETE
- Evidence:          concerns.md:17-24 fixes net/http, encoding/csv, parquet-go, testify — but no 02-architecture/.
- Destination:       ADRs + Solution Architecture
- Deliverable:       ADR-001 Parquet writer choice; ADR-002 CLI-vs-daemon execution model; ADR-003 per-payer mapping format.
- Next workflow mode: design

### G8 — No Technical Design for FEAT-001
- Classification:    INCOMPLETE
- Evidence:          No 03-design/ artifact; FEAT-001:23-28 names "quarantine file format documented" with nothing to point at.
- Destination:       Technical Design (FEAT-001)
- Deliverable:       Canonical schema, schema-mapping.yaml, quarantine CSV format, CLI surface, manifest, dedup key, audit cols (resolves G1), observability hooks (resolves G4).
- Next workflow mode: design

### G9 — No Test Plan binding FEAT-001 acceptance to checks
- Classification:    INCOMPLETE
- Evidence:          FEAT-001:23-28 names acceptance (golden round-trip, CI on new payers, stable quarantine).
- Destination:       Test Plan (FEAT-001)
- Deliverable:       Golden-CSV fixture, per-payer mapping fixtures, round-trip + quarantine assertions, R-5 throughput micro-bench.
- Next workflow mode: design

### G10 — No Implementation Plan / tracked work
- Classification:    INCOMPLETE (blocked on G7-G9)
- Evidence:          No 05-implement/ or bead tracker.
- Destination:       Implementation Plan / beads
- Deliverable:       Ordered, sized work items derived from the TD: parser, mapper, validator, dedup, parquet-writer, quarantine, manifest, CLI, observability.
- Next workflow mode: polish (after design produces TD).

## Suggested execution order

1. validate G5 — trivial fix inside FEAT-001.
2. frame   G1, G2, G3, G4 — close PRD gaps before architecture.
3. design  G7 → G8 → G9 — ADRs, then TD, then TP.
4. polish  G10 — derive beads from TD/TP once they exist.

narration: Ten gaps, each with destination, deliverable, next mode, and evidence. The next agent in the loop knows where to write, what shape it owes, and what to cite.
